characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: An eleven-year-old boy and titular character of the series. All-Loving Hero: Elliott is unwaveringly kind and friendly, even to his enemies. Blue Is Heroic: His main outfit he's seen wearing is a blue sweater with Saturn on it. Cheated Angle: His bangs always face the direction furthest from the camera. Fish Out of Water: Has some difficulty adjusting to the alien technology and culture of The Centrium, although thanks to his nomadic background he's able to adapt fairly quickly.

characters.name: Elliott
characters.description: An eleven-year-old boy and titular character of the series. All-Loving Hero: Elliott is unwaveringly kind and friendly, even to his enemies. Blue Is Heroic: His main outfit he's seen wearing is a blue sweater with Saturn on it. Cheated Angle: His bangs always face the direction furthest from the camera. Fish Out of Water: Has some difficulty adjusting to the alien technology and culture of The Centrium, although thanks to his nomadic background he's able to adapt fairly quickly.

characters.name: Frankie
characters.description: A geologist and mother to Elliott. She aspires to uncover new geological discoveries by researching geology from space. The Glasses Gotta Go: She starts the series off in a pair of blue glasses which are later given to Mo to help him see better. They eventually get replaced by a red pair. Good Parents: Needless to say, she takes living in space rather well, isn't terribly worried about her son going off on his own but still watches over him when needed, and her close relationship with Elliott is based on mutual teamwork and respect. Horrible Judge of Character: In "Wednesday" (the pilot episode), she doesn't take Elliott's suggestion to follow the creepy landlord willing to give them a home because, well, he's creepy. But when a (seemingly) nice old lady invites them to her house and Frankie is okay with it, the trio almost gets turned into statues by the old lady. Really Moves Around: Because of her profession, she and her son have to move on a regular basis.

characters.name: Mo
characters.description: AStegosaurusElliott and Frankie meet in a biosphere. Accessory-Wearing Cartoon Animal: He only wears glasses. Artifact Name: Mo gains his name shortly after meeting Elliott and Frankie. It's inspired by "Maureen", what Elliott's mothers planned to name him if he had been born a girl. Blind Without 'Em: Prior to trying on Frankie's glasses, his vision was terribly blurry. Literal-Minded: So much that when Frankie advises him to "look but don't touch" stuff has him think touching stuff is fine while his eyes are shut. Much of his thought process works like this to at least some extent.

characters.name: Lord Kallous the Merciless
characters.description: A retired supervillain who serves as Elliott and Mo's teacher. Big Bad: He's the closest thing to a main antagonist the show has and the only recurring villain, especially since most episodes haveNo Antagonist. Expy: His design is clearly based on Skeletor. Retired Monster: He's a former planet-conquering warlord and while he doesn't do much active supervillainy, he makes no attempt to hide the fact he's still evil. Sadist Teacher: Expected given he's a retired supervillain.
